module_role: Module 5 of v17 integration — per-settlement governance state (Order/Prosperity/Defense), canonical §3.2 governance-action Ob formulas, §4.3 settlement-events table, RM Cell Resilience (ED-683 / §3.3), settlement -> territory aggregation at arc boundary. Pure primitives — does not mutate world state.

entries.quoted_text: Order 0 | Local revolt (analogous to province Accord 0 but at settlement scale)

entries.section: §3.2 Governor Assignment table Pacify row + §4.3 Order 5 row
entries.quoted_text: Pacify | Charisma + local History | floor((3 − Order) + 1), min 1 | Order +1 (cap: 5). Order 5 + Prosperity 4+ | Flourishing
Prosperity 0 | Famine or economic collapse
Defense 0 + adjacent hostile military | Raid or siege
Prosperity 0 | Famine or economic collapse. Population leaving. Order −1 automatic.
Order −1 automatic
Order 0 | Local revolt (analogous to province Accord 0 but at settlement scale). Governor expelled unless garrison present.
Order 5 + Prosperity 4+ | Flourishing. Local festival, trade fair, or cultural event. +1 Disposition with all local NPCs.
Order 5 + Prosperity 4+ | Flourishing
+1 Disposition with all local NPCs
Mine type + Prosperity 3+ | Resource surplus. Province Treasury +50/season at Accounting (economic contribution, derived_stats_v1).
Province Treasury +50/season at Accounting
Fortress type + hostile military in province | Garrison mobilization. Defense check: Defense pool vs Ob 2. Success: settlement holds. Failure: attacker bypasses or captures.

entries.section: §3.3 Subnational Faction Governance — RM Cell Resilience (ED-683)
entries.quoted_text: If RM has Presence markers in ≥ 3 settlements within a province, Church/Crown suppression actions against RM in that province take +1 Ob.

entries.section: §3.3 Subnational Faction Governance — RM Cell Resilience
entries.quoted_text: Church/Crown suppression actions against RM in that province take +1 Ob
Emergency Domain Actions (Muster, Fortify, Emergency Diplomacy) in RM settlements take +1 season to resolve.
Waivable: RM leader spends 1 Mandate + loses 1 Presence marker in that province
loses 1 Presence marker in that province (community perceives consensus violation)

entries.section: §4.3 Settlement Events RM takes control row
entries.quoted_text: Player (or Vossen if NPC) chooses: Disestablishment (remove existing governance infrastructure; −1 Order for 2 seasons, then Accord growth +0.5/season; PT −1 immediate); Accommodation (maintain existing infrastructure under RM oversight; no penalty, PT drops 0.5 only, standard Accord); Transformation (4-season gradual conversion; no penalty during transition; PT −1 and Accord +0.5/season after completion).
Develop | Cognition + Wealth-relevant History | floor(Prosperity/2) + 1 | Prosperity +1 (cap: settlement type max)
Fortify | Military-relevant stat + History | floor(Defense/2) + 1 | Defense +1 (cap: settlement type max)
Pacify | Charisma + local History | floor((3 − Order) + 1), min 1 | Order +1 (cap: 5)
Administer | Attunement + Governance History | 2 | Maintain Order (no decay this season). Reveals one local NPC's active Conviction.
Prosperity +1 / Defense +1 / Order +1 / Maintain Order (no decay this season)
Define settlement → territory aggregation: settlement state → per-territory Accord/PT/infrastructure
entries.assumption: M5_ASSUMPTION_TWO: canon does not specify the aggregation function; floor(mean) is the conservative interpretation.

entries.quoted_text: settlement state → per-territory Accord/PT/infrastructure
entries.assumption: M5_ASSUMPTION_FIVE: continuous mean-based aggregation supplements canon's flat per-Mine +50/season rate. Both surfaces exposed; integration callers choose which to use.

entries.quoted_text: Provisional rate per M5_ASSUMPTION_FIVE; canon specifies the flat per-Mine +50/season but not a continuous conversion.

entries.value: {sid, event_type, severity, immediate_effects, choice_required, choice_options, suppressed_by_garrison, description}
entries.section: §4.3 Settlement Events — feeds Scene Slate at Priority 4 (Territorial)
entries.quoted_text: Each settlement generates 0–1 local events per season based on its stats and type. These feed into the Scene Slate at Priority 4 (Territorial).

provisional_assumptions.topic: Governance state lives in a separate dataclass
provisional_assumptions.decision: SettlementGovernance dataclass owns Order/Prosperity/Defense plus rm_presence, garrison_present, governor_faction, rm_governed flags. M1's Settlement class is preserved as church-infrastructure-only.
provisional_assumptions.rationale: M1's Settlement is narrow per its design — four church-infrastructure axes. Adding governance state would dilute that focus. Separate dataclass preserves separation of concerns; integration module wires both onto the same sid.
provisional_assumptions.follow_up: M7 integration must build the governance map alongside the M1 registry at world construction time.

provisional_assumptions.decision: aggregate_order_to_accord uses floor(mean(settlement.order for settlement in province)). Function exposed for override.
provisional_assumptions.rationale: Canon §5 Phase 5 specifies that settlement state feeds territory state but does not specify the aggregation function. Floor-mean is conservative (preserves discrete Accord values, rounds toward instability), gives clean integer Accord targets.
provisional_assumptions.follow_up: M7 balance sweep may reveal that floor-mean under-represents settlements with high Order. Consider min-max-clipping, weighted (population-weighted), or median alternatives. Document tradeoffs in M7.

provisional_assumptions.decision: rm_presence is a boolean flag on SettlementGovernance. RM Cell Resilience threshold (>=3 settlements within a province) sums booleans within a province via M1's territory_settlements helper.
provisional_assumptions.rationale: Canon §3.3 ED-683 says 'Presence markers in >= 3 settlements within a province'. A boolean marker per settlement is the minimal sufficient representation. Multi-tier presence strength is out of scope for M5.
provisional_assumptions.follow_up: If multi-tier RM presence becomes mechanically relevant (e.g., 'strong vs weak Presence'), expand to a counter/enum.

provisional_assumptions.topic: resolve_settlement_events returns event records, does not mutate state
provisional_assumptions.decision: Primitives module returns a list of SettlementEvent objects. Caller (integration module) applies immediate_effects to world state at arc boundary. Governance Transition scenes return choice_options for player/Vossen selection.
provisional_assumptions.rationale: Keeps M5 pure-primitives consistent with the other v17 modules. State mutation belongs in the integration layer where world has the necessary context (which faction is doing what, what the calendar phase is).
provisional_assumptions.follow_up: M7 integration applies events. Caller must handle the Governance Transition choice via a dispatch mechanism (likely npc_behavior_v30 §8 for NPC factions, player UI for player factions).

provisional_assumptions.decision: Two surfaces: (1) flat per-Mine MINE_SURPLUS_TREASURY_DELTA=+50 per Mine at Prosperity 3+ (canon §4.3), and (2) continuous aggregate_prosperity_to_wealth = mean(Prosperity) × 0.1 per province (provisional rate).
provisional_assumptions.rationale: Canon specifies the flat per-Mine rule explicitly. The continuous rate is needed because integration_plan §5 Phase 5 calls for general settlement-state-to-territory-state aggregation, not just Mine-specific. The 0.1 rate is provisional and should be calibrated empirically in M7.
provisional_assumptions.follow_up: M7 balance sweep should report wealth contribution distribution per faction and adjust PROSPERITY_TO_WEALTH_RATE if any faction is materially under- or over-served. Alternative: deprecate the continuous rate and use only the per-Mine flat rate.
